methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rhoman Aerospace is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Thomas Youmans and Thomas Callen. The valuation universe comprises 8 private companies operating in this space, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Peer-median valuation is not applicable to this analysis. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only 4 same-sector peers total, insufficient to establish a reliable comparable set. Specifically, no seed-stage peers, no adjacent-stage peers, and no similar-tier peers could be identified to meet the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints required for triangulation. Relying on all-sector median multiples would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and capital intensity across the broader drones and autonomous systems landscape.

Without a sufficiently dense comparable universe at Rhoman's stage and scale, this analysis cannot support a quantified valuation range at this time. The primary uncertainty is the lack of observable market prices for seed-stage autonomous systems companies. Resolution would require either: identification of additional seed or early-stage transactions in this subsector, or deployment of alternative methods (e.g., discounted cash flow or venture capital method) using company-specific financial projections and risk assumptions not provided in the current dataset.
